chunks.en: Today I want to talk about washing the dishes at home.
chunks.ja: 今日は家での皿洗いについて話したいと思います。

chunks.en: I usually wash the dishes right after dinner, so they don't pile up.
chunks.ja: 私はたいてい夕食のすぐ後に皿を洗うので、ためこまずに済みます。

chunks.en: I use a sponge, some soap, and hot water to clean everything.
chunks.ja: スポンジと洗剤とお湯を使って、全部きれいに洗います。

chunks.en: The dishes I hate washing most are the oily pans from cooking.
chunks.ja: 一番洗いたくないのは、料理で使った油っぽいフライパンです。

chunks.en: I don't have a dishwasher, so I wash everything by hand.
chunks.ja: 食洗機がないので、全部手で洗っています。

chunks.en: In the end, washing the dishes is boring, but I feel good after.
chunks.ja: 結局、皿洗いは退屈ですが、終わった後は気分がいいです。

outline: Opening: talk about washing the dishes
Point 1: when and how I wash them
Point 2: hardest dishes and by hand

modelTalk.text: I usually wash the dishes right after I eat. I don't like to leave them for later. When there are many dishes, the kitchen feels messy. So I clean them fast. I use a soft sponge, some soap, and warm water. First, I put a little soap on the sponge. Then I rub each plate and cup well. Warm water helps a lot, and it takes the oil away. The dishes I hate most are the oily ones. Frying pans are the worst for me. The oil sticks, and it's really hard to clean. Sometimes I let the pan soak for a while. I don't have a dishwasher at home, so I wash everything by hand. It's not my favorite job, but it's quick. After that, I dry my hands and feel happy. My kitchen looks clean again.

chunks.en: I usually wash the dishes right after dinner, so they don't pile up.
chunks.ja: 私はたいてい夕食のすぐ後にお皿を洗うので、たまってしまわないようにしています。

chunks.en: The way I do it is simple: a sponge, some soap, and hot water.
chunks.ja: 私のやり方はシンプルで、スポンジと洗剤とお湯を使うだけです。

chunks.en: The dishes I hate washing most are greasy frying pans after cooking meat.
chunks.ja: 一番洗いたくない食器は、肉を焼いた後の油っぽいフライパンです。

chunks.en: I wash everything by hand because my small kitchen doesn't have a dishwasher.
chunks.ja: うちの狭いキッチンには食洗機がないので、私はすべて手洗いしています。

chunks.en: In the end, washing up is boring, but a clean sink always feels good.
chunks.ja: 結局、皿洗いは退屈ですが、シンクがきれいだといつも気持ちがいいです。

outline: Opening: when I wash the dishes
Point 1: how I do it with sponge and hot water
Point 2: dishes I hate and hand-washing
Wrap-up: boring but a clean sink feels good

modelTalk.text: I usually don't wash the dishes right after I eat. I'm too full and lazy, so I leave them in the sink. But if I wait too long, the food dries up and it's harder to clean. So most nights I wash them before bed. I use a soft sponge, some dish soap, and hot water, because hot water cuts through grease much better. The dishes I hate washing most are the greasy ones, like the frying pan after I've cooked bacon. The oil sticks everywhere, and it takes ages to get it off. I don't have a dishwasher, so I wash everything by hand. Honestly, I don't really mind it. It's kind of relaxing when I put on some music. When I'm done, I leave everything to dry, and my kitchen looks clean again.
